Tell me something interesting about black holes.
metadata.notes: TTS measured through synth (Piper + RVC) without playback. VRAM is total GPU 0 used (includes desktop apps); deltas are the meaningful figures.

per_query.first_sentence_text: A black hole's event horizon is not a surface but a boundary in spacetime; crossing it is irreversible, yet from an outside observer's perspective, time dilation prevents anything from ever appearing

scope.git_head_subject: Phase C / Phase 1: context projection refactor
scope.voice_path_baseline_source: Most recent run of scripts/measure_baseline.py (timestamp 2026-05-08T01:21:22 UTC) -- captured before this Foundation phase began. Re-using rather than re-running keeps GPU idle for ongoing development; the data is < 1 day old and HEAD has not changed since.

vram_ceiling_check.interpretation: voice_path_peak_mb_observed is ABOVE the 9.2 GB target but UNDER the 11.5 GB hard cap. This was the established baseline before the Foundation phase; this phase's no-regression rule is read against this number, not 9.2 GB. Search and coding-session measurements both stayed under the voice-path peak, confirming neither path adds local GPU load beyond what the LLM keeps resident for any voice query.

daemon_env_vars_tested.OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE: -1 (never unload; observed as max int64 ns sentinel)

vram_observation.interpretation: Ollama uses ~1.2 GiB more local VRAM for the same GGUF, even with flash-attn + q8_0 KV both on. Likely SSM-state allocation overhead specific to this hybrid qwen35 architecture. With Whisper + RVC + Piper added, projected voice-stack peak ~11534 MB, marginally over the 11500 MB hard cap.

latency_observation.interpretation: Small TTFT regression attributable to localhost HTTP + JSON encoding. Generation throughput comparable (~50-58 tok/s in either engine).

behavior_observation.llama_cpp_done_reason_typical: stop (model emits EOS at end of answer)
evidence:
llama-cpp '817.' vs Ollama '

To find the product of 19 and 43, we can use the standard multiplication method...'

llama-cpp 'No.' vs Ollama '

1. **Understanding the question** ...numbered breakdown'
Every Ollama response generated past assistant turn boundary, none stopped at EOS naturally
behavior_observation.root_cause: GGUF eos_token_id=248046 (<|im_end|>) but Unsloth-quantized model emits <|endoftext|> (id 248044) at turn ends. Ollama respects metadata EOS but model bypasses it. llama-cpp's create_chat_completion handles this differently (template-aware stops in OpenAI-compat wrapper). Multi-stop API parameter does NOT fix; old engine and new engine both exhibit the same broken stop behavior.

ollama_compat_test.conclusion: Ollama mechanically loads the qwen35 GGUF and runs it, but cannot reproduce llama-cpp-python's runtime behavior with this specific model. Migrating the voice pipeline to Ollama would change Ultron's voice character (verbose vs terse), break stop handling (every response hits length cap), and add ~1.2 GiB of local VRAM that puts the voice stack marginally over the 11.5 GB hard cap.

decision.openclaw_sharing_strategy: use llama-cpp-python's built-in OpenAI-compatible server (python -m llama_cpp.server) when OpenClaw integration arrives. Same loader, same model file, same Ultron behavior, both consumers share weights.
decision.config_implication_phase_3: Phase 3 unified config will use llm.provider='llama_cpp' (in-process) for this Foundation phase. Phase 3 may add llm.server.* keys for the future llama-cpp-server transition; voice pipeline keeps in-process loader until the OpenClaw integration prompt actually needs sharing.

machine_state_after_test.user_action_needed: relaunch Ollama tray from Start menu if you want the original setup back

test_suite.warnings_summary: All warnings are DeprecationWarning for get_session_state(); the projection refactor (commit 4ecc7ec) deprecated this MCP tool but kept it for backward compat. Removal scheduled for a later phase.
test_suite.skipped_tests_note: Skipped tests are gated on PYTEST_RUN_GPU_TESTS=1 (load real models) or PYTEST_RUN_MIC_TESTS=1 (real microphone). Not run in this baseline pass to avoid blocking GPU and metering Claude API.

search_query_vram.user_query: What are the latest features in Python 3.13?
search_query_vram.notes: Brave and Jina are mocked (no network). VRAM cost of the search hop is the LLM ranking call (one short prompt, ~128 max_tokens). The orchestrator would also issue a final-response LLM call afterward (not measured here -- already captured in voice-path per-query VRAM).

coding_session_vram.notes: Voice stack remains loaded throughout. The scripted mock bridge runs in a worker thread (no Claude subprocess). Real Claude Code runs as a SUBPROCESS and consumes negligible local GPU; the LLM hosting the answer is at Anthropic, not local. So this measurement validates that the orchestration machinery itself doesn't spike local VRAM.

time_to_acknowledgment.notes: Microbenchmark of AcknowledgmentSource.next_phrase() in isolation. End-to-end TTA also includes TTS synth (see phase_foundation_start.latency_ms.aggregate.tts_synth_ms; ~219-937 ms) and audio device output latency. The 200 ms Part-0.1 target is for the phrase being EMITTED to the TTS pipeline, not audible to user; phrase-selection is several orders of magnitude under the budget.

first_token_from_end_of_speech.notes: Composite estimate only. True end-of-speech-to-first-token needs VAD + Whisper + pre-flight + LLM streaming through the live orchestrator with mic input; this script runs headless. Composite ignores VAD silence-detection delay (MIN_SILENCE_DURATION_MS=500) and pre-flight latency (~50-200 ms when search-triggering). The number is therefore a LOWER BOUND for real-world TTFA.

coding_orchestration_scenarios.notes: Times are pytest's setup+call+teardown summed per test. Each scenario uses the scripted mock bridge (no Claude API). Variance run-to-run is dominated by Qdrant-embedded init (loaded once per scenario via UltronMCPServer constructor).

measurements_captured_via_option_B.metric: VRAM during search-triggering query (Brave + Jina active)
measurements_captured_via_option_B.reason_skipped: Not part of measure_baseline.py; would require instrumented search hop with mocked or real API calls.

measurements_captured_via_option_B.metric: VRAM during coding session (Claude Code subprocess active)
measurements_captured_via_option_B.reason_skipped: Requires invoking Claude Code subprocess (metered against user's API budget).
Requires instrumented voice pipeline run with search-trigger query.

tests/coding/test_orchestration.py runs the 10 scenarios but does not currently emit per-scenario timing into a baseline file. Needs instrumentation OR the user can authorize running tests/coding/test_orchestration_real.py with PYTEST_RUN_GPU_TESTS=1 (metered via Claude API).

Existing baselines measure first_token_ms from the moment the LLM stream is invoked (post-Whisper). The end-of-speech-to-first-token timing requires the real audio VAD path -- runs through the orchestrator with mic input.

phase_0_openclaw_integration.note: Partial baseline: VRAM idle + OpenClaw inventory captured autonomously. Voice-query + OpenClaw-turn VRAM and first-token latency require user-approved interactive model loads.

doctor_findings: 1/1 recent sessions missing transcripts (history will appear to reset).

tests.note: From this worktree at HEAD = origin/main + Phase 4 deferred wrappers (still uncommitted).
